name: LSM Tree
def: A write-optimised store buffering in memory and flushing immutable sorted runs merged in the background.
problem: Sustain very high write throughput on disk/SSD where random in-place updates are expensive.
mechanism: Writes hit an in-memory memtable + WAL; flushed to immutable sorted SSTables; background compaction merges runs. Reads check memtable then levels, aided by Bloom filters.
limits: Read and space amplification from multiple levels
Compaction consumes I/O and can cause latency spikes
failures: Compaction falling behind → read amplification blow-up
Write stalls when memtable flush can’t keep up
myths: "Always beats B-trees" — only for write-heavy; reads pay amplification
